description: DBS=0 vs DBS=1 stratified iter5 (T3) and iter12 (T1) LOOCV metrics, computed from per-subject predictions in lockbox JSONs cross-referenced against cv_dbs in ablation_v3_features.csv
computed_by: post-hoc stratification of pre-registered iter5/iter12 lockbox per-subject predictions; no model refitting
interpretation: T3 iter5 CCC is essentially identical across DBS strata (0.499 vs 0.496) — the model performs comparably on DBS-implant vs non-DBS subjects in rank concordance terms. MAE is higher in DBS=1 (8.72 vs 7.16) primarily because DBS subjects have higher mean severity (28.65 vs 23.12 UPDRS-III). Within-stratum calibration slopes (0.71, 0.75) are markedly steeper than the joint slope (0.402): pooled slope is dragged down by between-group mean shift, a Simpson-paradox-like effect. T1 iter12 shows a modest CCC gap (0.643 vs 0.555) suggesting DBS subjects with higher axial+truncal severity are slightly harder to predict per-item; MAE difference is small (1.47 vs 1.83). The substantive conclusion: cv_dbs as a Stage-1 covariate produces comparable concordance across DBS strata for T3, but the calibration story is non-trivial and the joint slope of 0.40 reflects between-group mean shift more than within-group compression.
